description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that defines a default artifact collecting any files matching build/test* on failure, and a default before_script that installs development packages via dnf (gcc, gcc-c++, gcc-gfortran, openblas, openblas-devel, lapack, lapack-devel, mpich, mpich-devel, openmpi, openmpi-devel, hdf5, hdf5-devel, git, make, cmake, python, python3, python3-pip, python3-h5py, redhat-rpm-config, findutils, which, procps-ng, coreutils, environment-modules), sources the bash and module profiles, purges and lists modules, prints system info (uname -a, lscpu, free -h), shows compiler versions (gfortran, gcc, g++), loads the openmpi and mpich modules and prints their mpirun versions, and finally prints python, git and cmake versions. Set the job tags to the value of the RUNNER_TAG variable. Include the local CI files .gitlab-ci/.functions.yml, .gitlab-ci/.serial.yml, .gitlab-ci/.openmpi.yml, .gitlab-ci/.mpich.yml, .gitlab-ci/.intel.yml, .gitlab-ci/.coverage.yml, and .gitlab-ci/.nightly.yml. Define variables GET_SOURCES_ATTEMPTS=10, GIT_SSL_NO_VERIFY=true, GIT_STRATEGY=fetch, GIT_SUBMODULE_STRATEGY=recursive. Add a workflow with rules: when the pipeline source is a merge request event and the project namespace is 'dalton', set RUNNER_TAG to saas-linux-2xlarge-amd64; otherwise, for any other namespace, set RUNNER_TAG to saas-linux-medium-amd64.
